text: In order to advance the progress of Circular Economy (CE) research and contribute to climate change adaptation, the current study aims to develop a preliminary research agenda for the development of CE in African countries. It intends to review the state of knowledge of CE, while also identifying gaps in CE research and barriers to circular economy research in Africa. The research methodology employed is a mixed method research design. In this study, a scoping literature review of a total of 275 English and French articles from Google Scholar were used. This was combined with a quantitative and qualitative survey with 38 experts from the Circular Economy industry. These experts were found to be currently engaged in different CE projects in African countries. Findings revealed that South Africa is in the lead position of CE research with the most relevant number of articles and CE projects undertaken by CE experts. It therefore becomes an imperative call for scholars to conduct country-specific research on the CE developments for the other African countries as the available number of articles do not indicate any discrimination between the countries. The existing body of knowledge on CE as revealed in the journal papers is still focused on designing out waste and pollution by circulating products and materials; (keep products and materials in use). This indicates the presence of significant gaps in the other principles of CE such as designing waste and pollution out of the production processes and also regenerating natural systems. Some potential research topics that need further investigation are also listed. The study hereby attempts to establish a preliminary research agenda for the development of CE in Africa and it is one of a kind studies on this subject as far as the authors know.
